meta.source: Hana-san directive: refine pr-split, automated splitting with Claude Code integration, deep integration tests, cull AI slop. Incorporating ALL 19 issues from scratch/issues.md in logical order AFTER the integration test.
meta.maintenance: Update incrementally after every sub-task. Deviations logged within the plan.
meta.CRITICAL_SEQUENCE_RULE: THE INTEGRATION TEST (T052) MUST ALWAYS REMAIN FIRST IN SEQUENTIAL TASKS. THIS IS NON-NEGOTIABLE. THE IDIOT AI THAT WAS SUPPOSED TO IMPLEMENT THE INTEGRATION TEST FIRST ALREADY FAILED BY BEING A LAZY DOG. DO NOT REORDER THIS SEQUENCE UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCES. ANY ATTEMPT TO MOVE THE INTEGRATION TEST AWAY FROM POSITION 1 IS A TERMINABLE OFFENSE.

globalAlerts: ALL checks must pass: make (build + lint + test). No exceptions.
Platform compat: Linux, macOS, Windows. Tests use t.TempDir(), t.Setenv().
Integration test config via TestMain flags, project.mk targets with ?= variables.
No estimates, no priorities — 100% completion required.
DO NOT modify unrelated code. DO NOT remove existing comments/documentation.
Auto-split TUI must not hang. Cancellation must propagate immediately.
mandatoryDirectives: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start.
Update blueprint.json status after every sub-task.
Checkpoint WIP.md after every string of changes.
Rule of Two: 2 contiguous issue-free review cycles before commit.

statusSection.goalState: osm pr-split provides a fully functional automated splitting pipeline where: (1) `claude spawn` + `claude` works without EAGAIN — RunPassthrough defensively sets fd to blocking mode; (2) MCP session pre-registration is robust — reportClassification auto-creates sessions if missing; (3) Enter key delivery to Claude is reliable via single-write with \n appended; (4) terminal mux re-renders cleanly after toggle via in-memory VT100 virtual screen buffer; (5) integration tests with real AI (via TestMain + project.mk) cover spawn→switch→send→classify end-to-end; (6) all existing tests pass; (7) permission popups are handled via --dangerously-skip-permissions. ALL 19 ISSUES FROM issues.md ARE NOW ADDRESSED IN SEQUENTIAL TASKS T053-T071.
statusSection.currentState: T051 completed (make passes). T052 integration test target is next (MUST REMAIN FIRST - NON-NEGOTIABLE). T053-T071 contain all 19 issues from scratch/issues.md: UTF-8 handling, OSC terminator, SGR parsing, unbounded memory, ESC in CSI, PTY goroutine leak, VTerm data race, BubbleTea race, background starvation, erase rendition, status bar wipe, scroll region, cursor visibility, render inefficiency, swappedOnce reset, DECSET 1049 semantics, alt-screen variants, saved cursor bounds, dependency injection bypass.
replanLog: T006-T009 discovered already implemented (TestMain, integration tests, project.mk all existed). T013 simplified to loadStrategyPlugin removal only.
T021-T025 added: fix handle.send() blocking, fix ctrl+] blank terminal, sendToHandle wrapper, integration tests for new code.
T026-T028 added: fix PTY deadlock root cause (Write mutex), add timeout guard to sendWithCancel, termtest PTY integration tests.
T029-T042 added: fix MCP session pre-registration (BLOCKER), fix Enter key two-write pattern (BLOCKER), add --dangerously-skip-permissions (BLOCKER), update all affected tests, end-to-end validation.

T038-T053 REPLAN: Manual testing revealed T029-T037 fixes are insufficient. EAGAIN on stdin (non-blocking fd), MCP session still not found, Enter key unreliable, mux rendering broken. Complete rewrite of affected areas with integration test coverage.
T054-T072 ADDED: ALL 19 issues from scratch/issues.md incorporated as T053-T071 in logical order after T052 integration test. Sequence is NON-NEGOTIABLE - integration test MUST be first.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full make (build + lint + test) and fix all compilation and test failures from T038-T050
sequentialTasks.description: Run `make` to verify all changes compile, lint cleanly, and all tests pass. Fix any issues found. Ensure no deadcode warnings for new functions. Verify all new files have correct build tags.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make` completes with zero errors. All tests pass. No lint warnings. No deadcode findings.

sequentialTasks.task: Update project.mk integration-test-prsplit target to include new integration tests
sequentialTasks.description: Verify the existing integration-test-prsplit target in project.mk correctly runs the new integration tests (TestIntegration_ClaudeSpawnSwitch, TestIntegration_RealClaude_ClassificationE2E). Ensure the -run regex matches. Add a separate target `integration-test-prsplit-mcp` for the MCP-only tests (TestIntegration_MCPInstanceSession) that don't require real AI.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make integration-test-prsplit` runs all AI-requiring pr-split integration tests. `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` runs MCP-only tests (no AI needed). Both targets respect config.mk overrides.
sequentialTasks.PRIORITY_NOTE: THIS TASK MUST REMAIN FIRST. THIS IS NON-NEGOTIABLE. DO NOT MOVE IT.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement proper UTF-8 decoding in VTerm with carry buffer and wide character support
sequentialTasks.description: Fix VTerm.Write() to implement UTF-8 decoding stateful across calls using utf8.DecodeRune / utf8.FullRune with a carry buffer. Handle multi-byte UTF-8 characters (1-4 bytes) as single runes with correct column width. Handle wide characters (CJK, emojis) that occupy 2 columns. Update putChar() to accept rune instead of byte, track column position correctly for wide chars, and fix Render() to use string encoding instead of buf.WriteRune() to avoid mojibake.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: VTerm correctly processes multi-byte UTF-8 characters as single display units. Emoji (🤖) occupies 1 column (or 2 for wide chars), not 4. Cursor positioning remains correct after UTF-8 content. No mojibake on Render(). Wide character handling produces correct column advancement.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix OSC String Terminator handling in VTerm parser
sequentialTasks.description: Fix processOSC() to transition to stateEscape (not stateGround) when ESC (0x1B) is encountered inside an OSC sequence. This ensures the ST (String Terminator) byte (backslash) is consumed properly and not printed as a spurious character. Handle both BEL (0x07) and ESC\ (0x1B 0x5C) terminators correctly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: OSC sequences using ST termination (OSC 52/clipboard, OSC 8/hyperlinks, iTerm2 sequences) no longer leave visible backslash characters on screen. The ESC byte properly transitions to escape state, not ground state.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix SGR parsing to prevent attribute bleed on malformed sequences
sequentialTasks.description: Fix extended color handling (case p == 38 for truecolor) to properly fast-forward or abort when truncated sequences are detected. When i+3 >= len(params) for truecolor, do not allow parameter bleeding - either skip the malformed portion or abort the sequence entirely.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Truncated truecolor sequences like \x1b[38;2;1m do not cause the final parameter to be incorrectly evaluated as a separate SGR attribute. Malformed sequences are handled gracefully without attribute pollution.

sequentialTasks.task: Add bounds to paramBuf to prevent unbounded memory allocation
sequentialTasks.description: In the CSI/ESC parameter parsing, add a cap to len(v.paramBuf) (e.g., 256 bytes). If the buffer exceeds this limit, drop the malformed sequence and reset state to ground. This prevents potential OOM from unbounded buffer growth on malformed output without valid terminating bytes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Malformed CSI sequences without valid terminators cannot cause unbounded paramBuf growth. Memory usage remains bounded even with adversarial input.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix ESC handling in CSI state to properly transition to escape state
sequentialTasks.description: Fix processCSI() to transition to stateEscape (not stateGround) when ESC (0x1B) is encountered. This ensures aborted CSI sequences are properly handled and subsequent sequences are parsed correctly instead of being printed as literal characters.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Input like \x1b[3\x1b[1m (aborted CSI then new SGR) correctly parses [1m as bold instead of printing it as literal characters.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix PTY read goroutine lifecycle to prevent leaks and concurrent read contention
sequentialTasks.description: Fix RunPassthrough to prevent goroutine leaks. The current implementation uses os.File.Read which is a blocking syscall that ignores context cancellation. Either: (1) Implement a permanent background goroutine that starts on Attach and stops on Detach, with proper synchronization; (2) Set PTY FD non-blocking and poll with select/unix.Poll to respect context cancellation; (3) Close the PTY master on toggle (if acceptable). The solution must prevent multiple goroutines from competing to read from the same PTY fd.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Toggle cycles (attach->detach->attach) do not accumulate goroutines. Only one goroutine ever reads from the PTY at any time. Output is not fragmented or lost due to concurrent read contention.

sequentialTasks.task: Add mutex synchronization to VTerm to prevent data races and slice corruption panics
sequentialTasks.description: VTerm has zero internal synchronization. Add a mutex to VTerm or hold m.mu during entire Render() call. Resize() reallocates sb.cells while Write() and Render() access these slices concurrently. Also handle the race where RunPassthrough returns but goroutine may complete one more Read+Write cycle while next RunPassthrough calls Render() on the same VTerm.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: No data races detected by -race flag. No panic: runtime error: index out of range when resize runs concurrently with writes/renders. Concurrent access to VTerm state is properly serialized.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix BubbleTea async race condition with manual alt-screen management
sequentialTasks.description: Document and test the interaction between BubbleTea's async ReleaseTerminal() and the synchronous manual \x1b[?1049l escape sequence. Ensure that BubbleTea's delayed command does not fire during passthrough and corrupt terminal state. Consider the risk of RestoreTerminal overwriting passthrough's term.MakeRaw state with cooked mode, breaking toggle key interception.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Manual alt-screen sequences and BubbleTea async operations work correctly together. Toggle key interception remains functional. Terminal state is not corrupted by delayed BubbleTea commands.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix background process starvation when mux is in SideOsm state
sequentialTasks.description: When mux is in SideOsm (auto-split UI), nothing reads from m.child, causing the OS pipe buffer to fill and the child process to block on write(). Implement a permanent background goroutine that constantly reads from PTY, updates VTerm, and flushes to stdout only when passthrough is active.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Child processes do not freeze when mux is in SideOsm state. Background output is captured in VTerm, so toggle-back screen state is not stale.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix erase operations to preserve current rendition (background color) instead of using default attributes
sequentialTasks.description: Throughout eraseDisplay, eraseLine, deleteChars, insertChars, and scrolling new lines, use attr: sb.curAttr for blank cells instead of default/zero attributes. ECMA-48 specifies that erased/inserted blank cells should be filled with the current rendition including background color.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: TUIs that paint backgrounds by setting a background color and then clearing regions show correct colors on restoration. Erased regions preserve background color, not default attributes.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix status bar wiped on restore after first toggle
sequentialTasks.description: The status bar is written via renderStatusBar(h) but those bytes are not mirrored into childVterm (only child output is tee'd). After vt.Render() clears and re-renders, the status bar disappears. After rendering VTerm, explicitly re-render the status bar (if enabled) to restore it.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Status bar remains visible on second and subsequent toggles, not just the first.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix VTerm scroll region mismatch when status bar is enabled
sequentialTasks.description: When status bar is enabled, the real terminal scrolls within rows 1..h-1 (set via setScrollRegion), but VTerm's default scroll region is full-screen. Either: (1) Size VTerm to rows = h-1 when status bar enabled, paint status bar separately; (2) Explicitly set VTerm's scroll region to match the real terminal's scroll region.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: VTerm scroll behavior matches real terminal when status bar is enabled. Captured buffer accurately reflects what user saw, including scroll behavior at bottom of scroll region.

sequentialTasks.task: Restore cursor visibility state in Render()
sequentialTasks.description: Render() currently positions the cursor correctly but does not restore cursor visibility state (DECTCEM). If an application hid the cursor, restoration should reflect that. Add cursor visibility tracking and restore it in Render().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Applications that hide cursor (DECTCEM) have their visibility state correctly restored when toggling back to host TUI.

sequentialTasks.task: Optimize VTerm Render() to avoid generating CUP for empty lines
sequentialTasks.description: Render() currently generates CUP (cursor position) sequence for every row including empty ones. For large terminals, this is inefficient and may cause flicker. Add optimization to skip empty lines or trailing spaces, reducing output size and improving performance.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Render() output is smaller for terminals with empty lines. Performance improves for large terminals. No visual changes for non-empty content.

sequentialTasks.task: Reset swappedOnce flag in Detach() to ensure resize signal on new child
sequentialTasks.description: The swappedOnce flag ensures screen is cleared and child is resized only on first entry to passthrough mode. It is set in RunPassthrough but never reset in Detach(). When attaching a new child after detaching, the resize signal (SIGWINCH) is incorrectly skipped because swappedOnce is still true. Reset swappedOnce = false in Detach().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: New child processes receive resize signal on first toggle after attach. Terminal size is correctly initialized for new children.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix VTerm DECSET 1049 to save/restore alternate screen cursor correctly
sequentialTasks.description: When DECSET 1049 is invoked while already in alternate screen, it should save the alternate's cursor to the alternate's saved slot, not the primary's. Implementation currently always saves primary. Fix to track which screen (primary/alternate) is active and save/restore the correct cursor position.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Cursor position is correctly saved/restored when toggling between apps using DECSET 1049 multiple times or when 1049 is sent while already in alternate screen.

sequentialTasks.task: Add support for alt-screen mode variants (?47, ?1047) in addition to ?1049
sequentialTasks.description: Only DEC private mode ?1049 is currently implemented. Some programs use ?47 or ?1047 instead of (or in addition to) ?1049 for alternate screen. Add support for all common alt-screen variants in both set and reset modes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Applications using ?47 or ?1047 for alternate screen work correctly with passthrough mode.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix index out-of-bounds panic on saved cursor restore after resize
sequentialTasks.description: Resize() clamps curRow/curCol but neglects savedRow/savedCol. If child saves cursor (DECSC) at row 40, then terminal resizes to 24 rows, then child restores cursor (DECRC): curRow is blindly set to 40, causing panic: index out of range when Erase is called. Fix by clamping savedRow/savedCol in resize(), or clamping during DECRC/1049l restore.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: No panic when cursor is saved, terminal resized smaller, then cursor restored. Saved cursor position is properly clamped to new dimensions.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix dependency injection bypass in PrSplitCommand - use injected stdout instead of os.Stdout
sequentialTasks.description: Execute() takes stdout io.Writer for testability, but the toggle callback directly addresses os.Stdout for the \x1b[?1049l escape sequence. This causes integration tests capturing stdout to silently fail to register alt-screen transitions. Use the injected stdout parameter instead of os.Stdout.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All escape sequences for alt-screen toggle use the injected stdout writer. Integration tests that capture stdout correctly observe alt-screen transitions.

finalEnforcementProtocol: ALL make targets must pass before any commit.
Rule of Two: 2 contiguous subagent reviews required.
Integration tests must be verified with actual AI execution.
No hanging, no blocking, no screen artifacts in TUI.
